description: PrivateKey constructor must reduce values >= curve order n modulo n. Bug-31 in ts-sdk: values in the range [n, 2n) were accepted as-is instead of being reduced. Fixed by PrivateKey.fromString() wrapping mod-n before use.

regression.symptom: new PrivateKey(n + k) without the 'nocheck' flag accepted the out-of-range value and produced an incorrect WIF, different from PrivateKey(k)
regression.root_cause: Constructor did not reduce the scalar modulo the curve order before storing

vectors.description: n + 12 reduces to 12; both must produce the same WIF
n + 12 (overflow value) — fromString with implicit mod-n must also produce WIF of 12
vectors.skip_reason: Go-SDK parity gap (ts-sdk#31): Go-SDK v1.2.23 does not reduce scalars in the range [n, 2n) modulo the curve order; it stores the raw value and produces an incorrect WIF. TS SDK correctly reduces mod n. This vector documents the expected cross-language behaviour once Go-SDK is fixed.
vectors.notes: fffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffebaaedce6af48a03bbfd25e8cd036414d = secp256k1 curve order n + 12. TS SDK reduces mod n → 12. Go SDK (as of v1.2.23) does NOT reduce — returns the raw scalar. Marked intended: Go SDK parity needed.

vectors.description: Exact curve order n must be rejected as an invalid private key
vectors.skip_reason: Go-SDK parity gap (ts-sdk#31): Go-SDK v1.2.23 does not validate that the scalar is strictly less than the curve order n; it accepts n itself as a valid private key. TS SDK throws 'Input is out of field'. This vector documents the expected rejection behaviour once Go-SDK adds field membership validation.
vectors.notes: The curve order n itself is not a valid private key scalar. TS SDK throws. Go SDK (as of v1.2.23) accepts it. Marked intended: Go SDK should validate field membership.
